cards.front: Type 1 LSA
cards.back: Router LSA — every router originates one; lists its links and costs; floods only within its area

cards.front: Router LSA — every router originates one; lists its links and costs; floods only within its area
Type 2 LSA
cards.back: Network LSA — originated by the DR on multi-access segments; lists all routers attached to that segment; floods within area only

cards.front: Network LSA — originated by the DR on multi-access segments; lists all routers attached to that segment; floods within area only
Type 3 LSA
cards.back: Summary LSA — originated by ABR; advertises networks from one area into another; carries prefix + cost, NOT topology

cards.front: Summary LSA — originated by ABR; advertises networks from one area into another; carries prefix + cost, NOT topology
Type 4 LSA
cards.back: ASBR Summary LSA — originated by ABR; tells other areas how to reach an ASBR (so Type 5 routes are usable)

cards.front: ASBR Summary LSA — originated by ABR; tells other areas how to reach an ASBR (so Type 5 routes are usable)
Type 5 LSA
cards.back: AS External LSA — originated by ASBR; advertises routes redistributed from outside OSPF; floods through entire OSPF domain (except stub/NSSA areas)

cards.front: AS External LSA — originated by ASBR; advertises routes redistributed from outside OSPF; floods through entire OSPF domain (except stub/NSSA areas)
Type 7 LSA
cards.back: NSSA External LSA — ASBR inside an NSSA originates these; ABR converts Type 7 → Type 5 when leaving the NSSA

cards.front: NSSA External LSA — ASBR inside an NSSA originates these; ABR converts Type 7 → Type 5 when leaving the NSSA
Which LSAs stay inside one area (don't cross ABRs)?
Which LSAs are blocked by a stub area?
cards.back: Type 4 and Type 5 (external routes). Type 3 still allowed.

cards.front: Which LSAs are blocked by a totally stubby area?
cards.back: Type 3, 4, and 5. Only default route advertised in.

cards.front: NSSA allows [...] LSAs from a local ASBR, while still blocking Type 5 from other areas.
NSSA allows [...] LSAs from a local ASBR, while still blocking Type 5 from other areas.
ABR converts [...] LSAs into Type 5 when they leave an NSSA.
ABR converts [...] LSAs into Type 5 when they leave an NSSA.
![[lsa-flood-across-areas.svg]]
cards.back: Type 1 stays in originating area (intra). Type 3 re-originated by ABR to cross area boundaries. Type 5 originates at ASBR and floods entire OSPF domain (except stub/NSSA, where blocked).
